source: A Quick Review of E-PCM for Engineering Entrance - Physics
subject: Physics

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A wheel is at rest. Its angular velocity increases uniformly and becomes 80 radian per second after 5 second. The total angular displacement is:
questions.explanation: omega0=0, omega=80 rad/s, t=5s. alpha=(omega-omega0)/t=16 rad/s2. theta=omega0*t+0.5*alpha*t2=0+0.5*16*25=200 rad.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle of mass m begins to slide down a fixed smooth sphere from the top. What is its tangential acceleration when it breaks off the sphere?
questions.explanation: At break-off: mg costheta = mv2/R and v2=2gR(1-costheta). Solving gives costheta=2/3, sintheta=sqrt(5)/3. Tangential acceleration at=g sintheta=sqrt(5)g/3.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Let a_r and a_t represent radial and tangential acceleration. The motion of a particle may be circular if:
questions.explanation: For circular motion, centripetal (radial) acceleration a_r != 0 is necessary. a_t = 0 corresponds to uniform circular motion, which is a valid circular path.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle is moving along a circular path of radius 5 m with a uniform speed 5 ms-1. What will be the average acceleration when the particle completes half revolution?
questions.explanation: Change in velocity = 2v = 10 m/s (direction reversed). Time for half revolution = pi*r/v = 5*pi/5 = pi s. Average acceleration = 10/pi ms-2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 1 kg stone at the end of 1 m long string is whirled in a vertical circle at constant speed of 4 m/sec. The tension in the string is 6 N when the stone is at (g = 10 m/sec2):
questions.explanation: T = mg costheta + mv2/r. 6 = 10*costheta + 16/1 = 10*costheta + 16. So 10*costheta = -10, costheta = -1, theta = 180 degrees = top of circle.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The maximum tension that an inextensible ring of mass density 0.1 kg m-1 can bear is 40 N. The maximum velocity with which it can be rotated in a circular path is:
questions.explanation: T_max = (m/L)*V_max2. 40 = 0.1 * V_max2. V_max2 = 400. V_max = 20 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: If the banking angle of a curved road is given by tan-1(3/5) and the radius of curvature of the road is 6 m then the safe driving speed should not exceed (g = 10 m/s2):

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle moving along a circular path due to a centripetal force having constant magnitude is an example of motion with:
questions.explanation: Constant centripetal force magnitude implies constant speed. But velocity direction changes continuously, so velocity is variable. Hence: constant speed, variable velocity.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle of mass 2 kg is moving along a circular path of radius 1 m. If its angular speed is 2pi rad s-1, the centripetal force on it is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The speed of a particle moving in a circle is increasing. The dot product of its acceleration and velocity is:
questions.explanation: Speed is increasing means tangential acceleration is along velocity direction. a_t . v > 0. Centripetal acceleration is perpendicular to v (contributes 0). So a.v = a_t.v > 0 (positive).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle P is moving in a circle of radius a with a uniform speed u. C is the centre of the circle and AB is a diameter. The angular velocities of P about A and C are in the ratio:
questions.explanation: omega_C = v/r = u/a. By the inscribed angle theorem, angular velocity about A (endpoint of diameter) = omega_A = u/(2a). Ratio omega_A : omega_C = 1:2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A simple pendulum is vibrating with an angular amplitude of 90 degrees. For what values of alpha is the acceleration directed (i) vertically upwards (ii) horizontally (iii) vertically downwards?
questions.explanation: At alpha=0 (lowest): a_t=0, a_c is upward => vertically upward. At alpha=90 (extreme): v=0, a_c=0, a_t=g downward => vertically downward. When horizontal: tan(alpha)=a_c/a_t, a_c=2g*cos(alpha), gives cos(alpha)=1/sqrt(3).

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: When a body moves with a constant speed along a circle:
questions.optionC: no work is done on it
questions.optionD: no acceleration is produced in it
questions.explanation: Centripetal force is always perpendicular to velocity. Work = F.d*cos90 = 0. So no work is done by centripetal force.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two racing cars of masses m1 and m2 are moving in circles of radii r1 and r2 respectively. Their speeds are such that each makes a complete circle in the same time t. The ratio of the angular speeds of the first to the second car is:
questions.explanation: omega = 2*pi/T. Since both cars complete a circle in the same time T, omega1 = omega2. Ratio = 1:1.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A particle moves along a circle of radius (20/pi) m with constant tangential acceleration. If the velocity of the particle is 80 m/s at the end of the second revolution after motion has begun, the tangential acceleration is:
questions.explanation: Distance for 2 revolutions: s = 2 * 2*pi*(20/pi) = 80 m. v2 = u2 + 2*at*s => 6400 = 2*at*80 => at = 40 m/s2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: Two particles of equal mass revolving in circular paths of radii r1 and r2 respectively with the same angular velocity. The ratio of their centripetal force will be:
questions.explanation: F = m*omega2*r. Same mass and same omega: F is proportional to r. F1/F2 = r1/r2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A cyclist moving with a speed of 4.9 m/s on a level road can take a sharp circular turn of radius 4 m, then coefficient of friction between the cycle tyres and road is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A bucket tied at the end of a 1.6 m long string is whirled in a vertical circle. What should be the minimum speed so that the water from the bucket does not split when the bucket is at highest position?
questions.explanation: At highest point, v_min = sqrt(rg) = sqrt(1.6*10) = sqrt(16) = 4 m/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body is allowed to slide down a frictionless track freely under gravity. The track ends in a semicircular shaped part of a diameter D. The height (minimum) from which the body must fall so that it completes the circle, is:
questions.explanation: Radius r = D/2. Minimum height to complete loop: h_min = 5r/2 = 5*(D/2)/2 = 5D/4.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A stone of mass 1 kg tied to a light inextensible string of length L = 10/3 m, whirling in a circular path in a vertical plane. The ratio of maximum tension to the minimum tension in the string is 4. If g = 10 m/s2, the speed of the stone at the highest point is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A toy cart tied to the end of an unstretched string of length a, when revolved moves in a horizontal circle of radius 2a with a time period T. Now the toy cart is speeded up until it moves in a horizontal circle of radius 3a with period T'. If Hooke's law holds then:
questions.explanation: Spring extension a (radius 2a): k*a = m*(2pi/T)2*2a ...(i). Extension 2a (radius 3a): k*2a = m*(2pi/T')2*3a ...(ii). Dividing (i)/(ii): 1/2 = (T'2*2)/(T2*3). T'2 = (3/4)*T2. T' = (sqrt(3)/2)*T.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A body of mass 2 kg tied to the end of string of length 1 metre is whirled in a horizontal circle with a uniform angular velocity of 4 rad/sec. Then the tension of the string will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A motor car is travelling at 30 m/s on a circular road of radius 500 m. It is increasing its speed at the rate of 2 m/s2. Then acceleration of the car will be:
questions.explanation: a_c = v2/r = 900/500 = 1.8 m/s2. a_t = 2 m/s2. Total a = sqrt(1.82 + 22) = sqrt(3.24 + 4) = sqrt(7.24) = 2.69 approximately 2.7 m/s2.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A car of mass m moves in a horizontal circular path of radius r metre. At an instant its speed is v m/s and is increasing at a rate a m/s2, then the acceleration of the car is:
questions.explanation: Total acceleration = sqrt(a_t2 + a_c2) = sqrt(a2 + (v2/r)2). The tangential and centripetal accelerations are perpendicular to each other.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A small cone filled with water is revolved in a vertical circle of radius 4 m and the water does not fall down. What must be the maximum period of revolution?
questions.explanation: T_max = 2*pi*sqrt(r/g) = 2*pi*sqrt(4/10) = 2*pi*0.632 = 3.97 approximately 4 sec.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A cyclist goes round a circular path of circumference 34.3 m in sqrt(22) second. The angle made by him with the vertical will be:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A train has to negotiate a curve of radius 400 m. By how much height should the outer rail be raised with respect to inner rail for a speed of 48 km/hr? The distance between the rails is 1 m:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: A 500 kg car takes a round turn of radius 50 m with a speed of 36 km/h. The centripetal force is:

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The angular velocity of second hand of a clock is:
questions.explanation: Second hand completes one revolution in 60 s. omega = 2*pi/60 = pi/30 rad/s.

questions.subject: Physics
questions.text: The circular orbit of two satellites have radii r1 and r2 respectively (r1 < r2). If angular velocities of the satellites are same then their centripetal accelerations are related as:
questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration a = omega2*r. Same omega, so a is proportional to r. Since r1 < r2, a1 < a2.
